key_insight: PAC Studio ML is a human-in-the-loop, physics-informed ML environment that helps analyze ill-conditioned PAC spectra by narrowing site-count and parameter choices without replacing expert fitting.
engineering_relevance: It can speed inverse analysis, improve initialization for nonlinear refinement, and make PAC workflows more reproducible and diagnostically transparent for materials characterization.
method: Hamiltonian-based forward PAC modeling with user-defined synthetic training libraries
Feature extraction plus one-, two-, and three-site ML predictors for direct parameter estimation and site-family screening
ML-seeded nonlinear least-squares refinement with visualization, benchmarking, diagnostics, model cards, and export tools
evidence_signal: Held-out synthetic tests showed proof of operation and highlighted that some PAC parameters are much harder to recover than others; BiFeO3 examples demonstrated the main workflows as software case studies.
watch_item: The tool supports expert analysis but does not replace physical constraints, final model selection, or full experimental validation, so outputs should be treated as guidance rather than ground truth.
paper_title: PAC Studio Machine Learning: Human-in-the-Loop Analysis of TDPAC Spectra
